notes: Supported without restriction; anyone can initialize a Whirlpool with a TransferFeeConfig token. No TokenBadge required.
Supported without restriction (display/amount-scaling extension).
Supported without restriction (ScaledUiAmount; display/amount-scaling extension).
Listed under Supported (account-level extension).
Listed under Supported; also used by Whirlpools for position NFTs.
Listed under Supported; also leveraged by Whirlpools for position NFTs.
Supported as non-confidential transfer only — the mint may carry the extension but Whirlpools uses only the public transfer path.
Supported only if the token has an initialized TokenBadge (per-WhirlpoolsConfig whitelist). The hook is called after transfer processing and cannot modify the amount.
Supported only with an initialized TokenBadge; unrestricted pool creation was deemed too risky.
Supported only with an initialized TokenBadge; freeze authority should be disabled unless there is a reasonable reason.
Supported only with an initialized TokenBadge (whitelist).
Supported only with an initialized TokenBadge (added June 2025).
Listed explicitly under Not Supported.
Listed explicitly under Not Supported (Group, GroupPointer).
Group is listed under Not Supported (TokenGroup is the SPL implementation of Group).
Listed explicitly under Not Supported (Member, MemberPointer).
Member is listed under Not Supported (TokenGroupMember is the SPL implementation of Member).
Not in the supported list; falls under the page's catch-all 'all other extensions not listed above' = not supported.
Not named on the page; only non-confidential transfer is honored, so the confidential fee sub-extension falls under the catch-all.
The page states all extensions not explicitly listed are Not Supported, so any unrecognized extension blocks pool creation by default.
Orca Whirlpools uses a positive/closed support list with three tiers: freely supported, TokenBadge-gated (conditional), and not supported.
TokenBadge is a per-WhirlpoolsConfig PDA whitelist that permits pools for higher-risk extensions (TransferHook, PermanentDelegate, MintCloseAuthority, DefaultAccountState, Pausable).
Anything not on the supported list — including the catch-all 'all other extensions' — is treated as Not Supported. ConfidentialTransfer is honored only in non-confidential mode.
